HEARING RE: ORDER TO CAUSE FOR TEMPORARY/PRELIMINARY ORDER.THE COURT AND COUNSEL FOR PARTIES DISCUSS THE AGREEMENTS REACHED BY THE PARTIES REGARDING PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR A TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER. THE COURT SIGNS THE PARTIES STIPULATED TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER. PER THE AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES, THE COURT INDICATES BOTH ITS SEPTEMBER 10, 2019 ORDER AND THE TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER SIGNED TODAY ARE IN EFFECT ABSENT A FURTHER ORDER FROM THE COURT. THE COURT INDICATES THE PARTIES HAVE FURTHER AGREED TO E-MAIL THE COURT (AT CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG) BY THE CLOSE OF BUSINESS ON SEPTEMBER 26, 2019 AS TO 1.) THE STATUS OF THE ACTIVITIES REQUESTED BY THE TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER (INCLUDING THE TURNOVER AND THE FORENSIC ANALYSIS), AND 2.) THE PARTIES' REQUEST (IF ANY) FOR A FURTHER HEARING ON A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION OR STATUS CONFERENCE. (STIPULATED ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA A. TORREANO, CSR#8600, E-MAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/EPS)

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Feb-19-2020 Off Calendar

Status Conference Re: Further Analysis Of Forensic Imaging. (Added Per Approval Of The Court)
Off Calendar Nov-21-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Bradford K. Newman, Esq. of PAUL HASTINGS LLP, T:650.320.1865, for plaintiffs, on Court Call;
Shane K. Anderies, Esq. of ANDERIES & GOMES LLP T:415.217.8805, for defendant, on Court Call.

RULING: Status Conference Re: Further Analysis Of Forensic Imaging - Hearing was held.

Plaintiff's counsel requested the court's assistance regarding 2 issues: 1) Setting a deposition date and 2) Assigning a 3rd party to identify which files belong to the plaintiff. Defendant's counsel argues that defendant has complied with the court's orders and does not agree to allowing a 3rd party to look at defendant's privilege log.

The parties did not reach a resolution. The Court declined to offer advice or opinion or issue an order as nothing is before the the Court at this time. The Court merely provided his observations and some procedural guidelines regarding filing a discovery motion.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org

Status Conference Re: Further Analysis Of Forensic Imaging. (Added Per Approval Of The Court)
Off Calendar Oct-17-2019 Continued To Nov-21-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Bradford K. Newman, Esq. of Paul Hastings LLP, T:650.320.1865, for plaintiff, on court call;
Shane K. Anderies, Esq. of Anderies & Gomes LLP, T:415.217.8805, for defendants, on court call.

STATUS CONFERENCE RE: FURTHER ANALYSIS OF FORENSIC IMAGING

Hearing was held. The status of the case was discussed. Plaintiff's counsel reported that the parties are working collaboratively on forensic imaging and that he is still trying to determine if his client would want to move forward with preliminary injunction or engage in settlement discussion. Defendant's counsel wanted to discuss the deposition of his client Simon Burns and the potential criminal issues that could result from it.

After hearing, the Court sets a further status conference on November 21, 2019 at 9:30am. Parties may appear by court call. Parties to inform the clerk if resolution is reached before the hearing, or a hearing is no longer needed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Order To Cause For Temporary/Preliminary Order
Off Calendar Sep-12-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Attys. BRADFORD K. NEWMAN, ESQ. and JESSICA MENDELSON, ESQ. of Paul Hastings (Tel: 650-320-1827) appearing for Activehours, Inc. (d/ba Earnin);
Atty. SHANE K. ANDERIES, ESQ. of Anderies & Homes Employment Law (Tel: 415-205-9885) appearing for defendant Simon Burns.

Hearing Re: Order To Cause For Temporary/Preliminary Order.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed the parties to appear. The Court and counsel for parties discuss the agreements reached by the parties regarding plaintiff's request for a temporary restraining order. The Court signs the parties stipulated temporary restraining order. Per the agreement of the parties, the Court indicates both its September 10, 2019 order and the temporary restraining order signed today are in effect absent a further order from the Court. The Court indicates the parties have further agreed to e-mail the court (at contestdept302tr@sftc.org) by the close of business on September 26, 2019 as to 1.) the status of the activities requested by the temporary restraining order (including the turnover and the forensic analysis), and 2.) the parties' request (if any) for a further hearing on a preliminary injunction or status conference.

(Stipulated order signed in open court.)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano, CSR#8600, e-mail: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
